message:
Migrate pages/size, secret_scan, summary, wizard from rusqlite to frankensqlite

Port 4 pages modules and their tests to use frankensqlite compat layer:
- size.rs: Connection::open, query_row_map, ParamValue params
- secret_scan.rs: open_with_flags, query_with_params row iteration
- summary.rs: SummaryGenerator full migration (build_filter_clause,
  get_counts, get_time_range, get_date_histogram, get_workspace_summary,
  get_agent_summary, recalculate_with_exclusions)
- wizard.rs: Connection import swap
- e2e_pages.rs: Update test Connection opens and query_row calls
- sqlite.rs: Add FrankenStorage analytics methods (from prior session)

Also fix frankensqlite compile error: add missing order_by field to
AggColumn initializers in codegen.rs HAVING clause handler.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2aec","title":"P5.1: Secret Detection Scanner","description":"# P5.1: Secret Detection Scanner

**Parent Phase:** Phase 5: Polish & Safety
**Section Reference:** Plan Document Section 14 (Guardrail 3)
**Depends On:** P1.1 (Database Export)

## Goal

Before export, scan all conversation content for potential secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens, private keys) and alert the user.

## Technical Approach

### Secret Patterns (Regex)

```rust
use regex::Regex;
use lazy_static::lazy_static;

lazy_static! {
    static ref SECRET_PATTERNS: Vec<(&'static str, Regex)> = vec![
        // OpenAI API keys
        ("OpenAI API Key", Regex::new(r"sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{48}").unwrap()),
        
        // GitHub tokens
        ("GitHub PAT", Regex::new(r"ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}").unwrap()),
        ("GitHub OAuth", Regex::new(r"gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}").unwrap()),
        
        // AWS
        ("AWS Access Key", Regex::new(r"AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}").unwrap()),
        ("AWS Secret", Regex::new(r"(?i)aws[_-]?secret[_-]?access[_-]?key\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}").unwrap()),
        
        // Generic patterns
        ("API Key", Regex::new(r"(?i)api[_-]?key\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[\w-]{20,}").unwrap()),
        ("Secret", Regex::new(r"(?i)secret\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[\w-]{20,}").unwrap()),
        ("Password", Regex::new(r"(?i)password\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[^\s'\"]{8,}").unwrap()),
        ("Private Key", Regex::new(r"-----BEGIN (RSA |EC |DSA |OPENSSH )?PRIVATE KEY-----").unwrap()),
        
        // Anthropic
        ("Anthropic API Key", Regex::new(r"sk-ant-[a-zA-Z0-9-_]{40,}").unwrap()),
        
        // Generic high-entropy
        ("Bearer Token", Regex::new(r"(?i)bearer\s+[a-zA-Z0-9._-]{20,}").unwrap()),
    ];
}
```

### Entropy-Based Detection

```rust
/// Calculate Shannon entropy of a string
fn shannon_entropy(s: &str) -> f64 {
    let mut freq = [0u32; 256];
    let len = s.len() as f64;
    
    for b in s.bytes() {
        freq[b as usize] += 1;
    }
    
    freq.iter()
        .filter(|&&c| c > 0)
        .map(|&c| {
            let p = c as f64 / len;
            -p * p.log2()
        })
        .sum()
}

/// Detect high-entropy strings (potential secrets)
fn detect_high_entropy_secrets(content: &str) -> Vec<SecretMatch> {
    let mut matches = Vec::new();
    
    // Look for base64-like strings of 20+ chars
    let base64_re = Regex::new(r"[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}").unwrap();
    
    for m in base64_re.find_iter(content) {
        let s = m.as_str();
        let entropy = shannon_entropy(s);
        
        // High entropy (>4.5 bits per char) suggests random/secret data
        if entropy > 4.5 && s.len() >= 20 {
            matches.push(SecretMatch {
                pattern_name: "High-Entropy String".to_string(),
                matched_text: truncate_secret(s, 20),
                line_number: count_newlines(&content[..m.start()]) + 1,
                entropy: Some(entropy),
            });
        }
    }
    
    matches
}
```

### Scanner Implementation

```rust
pub struct SecretMatch {
    pub pattern_name: String,
    pub matched_text: String, // Truncated/masked
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub context: String,      // Surrounding text
    pub conversation_id: i64,
    pub message_idx: i64,
    pub entropy: Option<f64>,
}

pub struct ScanResult {
    pub matches: Vec<SecretMatch>,
    pub conversations_scanned: usize,
    pub messages_scanned: usize,
    pub scan_duration_ms: u64,
}

/// Scan all messages for potential secrets
pub fn scan_for_secrets(db: &Database, filter: &ExportFilter) -> Result<ScanResult> {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut all_matches = Vec::new();
    let mut msg_count = 0;
    let mut conv_count = 0;
    
    let conversations = db.query_filtered_conversations(filter)?;
    
    for conv in &conversations {
        conv_count += 1;
        let messages = db.get_conversation_messages(conv.id)?;
        
        for msg in messages {
            msg_count += 1;
            
            // Pattern matching
            for (name, pattern) in SECRET_PATTERNS.iter() {
                for m in pattern.find_iter(&msg.content) {
                    all_matches.push(SecretMatch {
                        pattern_name: name.to_string(),
                        matched_text: truncate_secret(m.as_str(), 20),
                        line_number: count_newlines(&msg.content[..m.start()]) + 1,
                        context: extract_context(&msg.content, m.start(), m.end(), 50),
                        conversation_id: conv.id,
                        message_idx: msg.idx,
                        entropy: None,
                    });
                }
            }
            
            // Entropy detection
            all_matches.extend(detect_high_entropy_secrets(&msg.content));
        }
    }
    
    Ok(ScanResult {
        matches: all_matches,
        conversations_scanned: conv_count,
        messages_scanned: msg_count,
        scan_duration_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
    })
}
```

### User Interface

```
⚠️  POTENTIAL SECRETS DETECTED

Scanned 2,035 conversations, 63,701 messages in 1.2s
Found 3 potential secrets:

  1. [OpenAI API Key] in conversation #1234, message #5
     Context: "...set OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-abc1...redacted..."
     
  2. [Password] in conversation #5678, message #12
     Context: "...password=SuperSec...redacted..."
     
  3. [High-Entropy String] in conversation #9012, message #3
     Context: "...token: eyJhbGciO...redacted..."
     Entropy: 5.2 bits/char

Options:
  [1] Exclude these conversations from export
  [2] Review each match individually
  [3] Redact secrets and continue (replace with [REDACTED])
  [4] Continue anyway (secrets will be encrypted)
  [5] Cancel export
```

## Test Cases

1. OpenAI key detected
2. GitHub PAT detected
3. AWS credentials detected
4. Private key detected
5. High-entropy string detected (entropy > 4.5)
6. False positives handled (UUIDs, hashes)
7. User-provided patterns work
8. Performance: 100K messages < 5 seconds

## Files to Create

- `src/pages/secrets.rs` (new)
- `src/pages/wizard.rs` (integrate scan step)

## Exit Criteria

1. All pattern types detected
2. Entropy detection works
3. User can choose action for each match
4. Scan completes in reasonable time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T05:26:52.518118526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T06:03:12.928872470Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T06:03:12.928872470Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of coding_agent_session_search-jk3m","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2aec","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-p4w2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}

PARENT EPIC: Complete frankensqlite Integration

WHAT: Port the core database operations in SqliteStorage to FrankenStorage using the compat adapter layer.

VERIFIED SCOPE: 13 files have rusqlite imports. Primary target: src/storage/sqlite.rs (~6,864 lines) contains the bulk.

VERIFIED RUSQLITE USAGE (grep counts 2026-02-27):
- query_row with closure: 118 total (sqlite.rs=57, lib.rs=23, plus 10 other files)
- query_map with closure: 64 total (sqlite.rs=14, analytics/query.rs=8, pages/analytics.rs=13)
- params![]: 96 total (sqlite.rs=61 plus 9 other files)
- execute_batch: 115 total (sqlite.rs=95 plus 9 other files)
- .optional(): 9 total (sqlite.rs=8, bookmarks.rs=1)
- .transaction(): 9 total (sqlite.rs=8, pages/export.rs=1)
- params_from_iter: 26 total (sqlite.rs=7, pages/summary.rs=10, plus 4 other files)
- Box<dyn ToSql>: 0 (NOT USED — corrected from prior estimate of 29)

CORE CRUD METHODS TO PORT (in sqlite.rs):
1. insert_conversations_tree() — bulk insert in transaction
2. get_conversation()/get_conversations() — query by ID/workspace/agent/date
3. get_messages()/get_message() — by conversation_id, role, content search
4. get_snippets() — by message_id
5. insert_indexed_file()/get_indexed_files() — index tracking
6. update_indexed_file() — re-index timestamp
7. delete_conversations_for_source() — cascading delete
8. get_conversation_count()/get_message_count() — aggregates
9. search_messages() — text search with LIKE
10. get_workspaces()/get_agents() — distinct lists

MIGRATION APPROACH (with compat adapter in place):
- Replace 'use rusqlite::...' with 'use frankensqlite::compat::...'
- Replace params![v1, v2] → compat params![v1, v2] (same syntax)
- Replace |row| row.get::<_, T>(i) → |row| row.get_typed::<T>(i)
- Replace conn.transaction() → compat transaction()
- Replace conn.execute_batch(sql) → compat execute_batch(sql)
- No Box<dyn ToSql> migration needed (0 occurrences)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2b1ebd48","title":"Index Freshness in Robot Meta","description":"# Index Freshness in Robot Meta

## Problem Statement
When searching, agents don't know if results might be incomplete due to stale index. They need this context to decide whether to re-index before trusting results.

## Proposed Solution
Extend `--robot-meta` to include freshness information:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --robot-meta
```

Output:
```json
{
  "_meta": {
    "elapsed_ms": 45,
    "cache_stats": {...},
    "wildcard_fallback": false,
    "index_freshness": {
      "last_indexed_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
      "age_seconds": 3600,
      "stale": true,
      "pending_sessions": 5
    }
  },
  "hits": [...]
}
```

## Design Decisions

### When to Include
Only when `--robot-meta` is specified (opt-in to avoid bloat).

### Staleness Warning
If index is stale, consider adding to top-level:
```json
{"_warning": "Index is 1 hour old; 5 sessions pending. Run 'cass index' for fresh results."}
```

### Performance
Freshness check should add <5ms:
- Read last_indexed_at from meta table (already connected)
- Read watch_state.json (fast file read)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--robot-meta` includes `index_freshness` object
- [ ] `stale` boolean accurately reflects state
- [ ] `pending_sessions` count is accurate
- [ ] Minimal performance impact (<5ms)

## Effort Estimate
Low - 1-2 hours. Builds on existing robot-meta infrastructure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.011862525Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T02:30:39.350423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-d6o","title":"P5.5 cass sources sync command","description":"# P5.5 cass sources sync command

## Overview
Implement the `cass sources sync` command that triggers synchronization
from configured remote sources and persists sync status.

## Implementation Details

### CLI Definition
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
pub enum SourcesCommand {
    /// Synchronize sessions from remote sources
    Sync {
        /// Sync only specific source(s)
        #[arg(long, short)]
        source: Option<Vec<String>>,
        
        /// Don't re-index after sync
        #[arg(long)]
        no_index: bool,
        
        /// Show detailed transfer information
        #[arg(long, short)]
        verbose: bool,
        
        /// Dry run - show what would be synced
        #[arg(long)]
        dry_run: bool,
    },
}
```

### Command Implementation
```rust
async fn cmd_sources_sync(args: &SyncArgs) -> Result<(), CliError> {
    let config = SourcesConfig::load()?;
    let engine = SyncEngine::new(config.clone());
    let mut status = SyncStatus::load()?;  // Load persisted sync status
    
    let sources_to_sync: Vec<_> = if let Some(names) = &args.source {
        config.sources.iter()
            .filter(|s| names.contains(&s.name))
            .collect()
    } else {
        config.sources.iter().collect()
    };
    
    if sources_to_sync.is_empty() {
        println!("No sources configured. Run 'cass sources add' first.");
        return Ok(());
    }
    
    let mut total_sessions = 0;
    for source in sources_to_sync {
        println!("Syncing {}...", source.name);
        
        let report = if args.dry_run {
            engine.dry_run_sync(source).await?
        } else {
            engine.sync_source(source).await?
        };
        
        // Update sync status (critical for P5.3 list display)
        if !args.dry_run {
            status.update(&source.name, &report);
        }
        
        print_sync_report(&report, args.verbose);
        total_sessions += report.sessions_found();
    }
    
    // Persist sync status
    if !args.dry_run {
        status.save()?;
    }
    
    // Trigger re-index
    if !args.no_index && !args.dry_run {
        println!("\nRe-indexing {} synced sessions...", total_sessions);
        reindex_remotes().await?;
    }
    
    Ok(())
}
```

### Sync Status Persistence
```rust
impl SyncStatus {
    pub fn load() -> Result<Self, io::Error> {
        let path = Self::status_path()?;
        if path.exists() {
            let content = std::fs::read_to_string(&path)?;
            Ok(serde_json::from_str(&content).unwrap_or_default())
        } else {
            Ok(Self::default())
        }
    }
    
    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<(), io::Error> {
        let path = Self::status_path()?;
        let content = serde_json::to_string_pretty(self)?;
        std::fs::write(&path, content)
    }
    
    pub fn update(&mut self, source_id: &str, report: &SyncReport) {
        self.sources.insert(source_id.to_string(), SourceSyncInfo {
            last_sync: Some(Utc::now()),
            last_result: if report.has_errors() {
                SyncResult::PartialFailure(report.error_summary())
            } else {
                SyncResult::Success
            },
            sessions_synced: report.sessions_found(),
        });
    }
    
    fn status_path() -> Result<PathBuf, io::Error> {
        Ok(dirs::data_local_dir()
            .ok_or_else(|| io::Error::new(io::ErrorKind::NotFound, "No data dir"))?
            .join("cass/sync_status.json"))
    }
}
```

### Output Format
```
Syncing laptop...
  ~/.claude/projects: 12 files updated (2.3 MB)
  ~/.cursor/projects: 0 files updated (up to date)
  Total: 47 sessions found
  
Syncing workstation...
  ~/.claude/projects: 3 files updated (156 KB)
  Total: 23 sessions found

Re-indexing 70 synced sessions...
Done. Run 'cass search --source=remote' to search remote sessions.
```

### Integration with Indexer
```rust
async fn reindex_remotes() -> Result<(), IndexError> {
    let remote_store = dirs::data_local_dir()?.join("cass/remotes");
    
    let mut scanner = SessionScanner::new();
    for entry in std::fs::read_dir(&remote_store)? {
        let entry = entry?;
        if entry.file_type()?.is_dir() {
            let source_name = entry.file_name().to_string_lossy().to_string();
            scanner.add_root_with_provenance(
                entry.path(),
                SourceType::Remote,
                Some(source_name),
            );
        }
    }
    
    scanner.scan_and_index().await
}
```

## Dependencies
- Requires P5.4 (sync engine)
- Requires P2.2 (indexer supports multiple roots)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `cass sources sync` syncs all configured sources
- [ ] `cass sources sync --source=laptop` syncs specific source
- [ ] Progress and stats shown during sync
- [ ] `--dry-run` shows what would happen
- [ ] Auto-reindex after sync (unless --no-index)
- [ ] Sync status persisted to sync_status.json

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-dbdl","title":"Implement 'cass sources setup' command","description":"# Implement 'cass sources setup' command

## What
Create the main `cass sources setup` command that orchestrates the entire 
remote sources setup workflow. This is the integration layer that ties together
all the component pieces into a polished user experience.

## Why
This is the "product" - the user-facing command that delivers the seamless 
multi-machine setup experience. All the other tasks build infrastructure; this
task builds the actual feature users interact with.

## Command Design

### CLI Definition
```rust
/// Interactive wizard to discover, configure, and set up remote sources
Setup {
    /// Preview what would happen without making changes
    #[arg(long)]
    dry_run: bool,
    
    /// Skip interactive prompts (use defaults)
    #[arg(long)]
    non_interactive: bool,
    
    /// Specific hosts to configure (skips discovery/selection)
    #[arg(long, value_delimiter = ',')]
    hosts: Option<Vec<String>>,
    
    /// Skip cass installation on remotes
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_install: bool,
    
    /// Skip indexing on remotes
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_index: bool,
    
    /// Skip syncing after setup
    #[arg(long)]
    skip_sync: bool,
    
    /// SSH connection timeout in seconds
    #[arg(long, default_value = "10")]
    timeout: u64,
    
    /// Continue from previous interrupted setup
    #[arg(long)]
    resume: bool,
    
    /// Show detailed progress output
    #[arg(long, short)]
    verbose: bool,
    
    /// Output as JSON (implies non-interactive)
    #[arg(long)]
    json: bool,
}
```

### Workflow Orchestration

```rust
pub fn run_sources_setup(opts: SetupOptions) -> CliResult<()> {
    // Load or create progress state
    let mut state = if opts.resume {
        SetupState::load()?.unwrap_or_default()
    } else {
        SetupState::default()
    };
    
    // Phase 1: Discovery
    if !state.discovery_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 1: Discovery");
        let discovered = discover_ssh_hosts();
        state.discovered_hosts = discovered.len();
        state.discovery_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
        
        if opts.dry_run {
            println!("  Would discover {} SSH hosts", discovered.len());
        } else {
            println!("  Found {} SSH hosts in ~/.ssh/config", discovered.len());
        }
    }
    
    // Phase 2: Probing (parallel)
    if !state.probing_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 2: Probing hosts");
        
        let progress = ProgressBar::new(state.discovered_hosts as u64);
        let probed = probe_hosts_parallel(&discovered, |completed, total, name| {
            progress.set_position(completed as u64);
            progress.set_message(name);
        })?;
        
        state.probed_hosts = probed.clone();
        state.probing_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
        
        // Show summary
        let reachable = probed.iter().filter(|p| p.reachable).count();
        let with_cass = probed.iter().filter(|p| p.cass_status.is_installed()).count();
        println!("  {} reachable, {} with cass installed", reachable, with_cass);
    }
    
    // Phase 3: Selection (interactive)
    let selected = if !state.selection_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 3: Host Selection");
        
        let selected = if opts.non_interactive || opts.hosts.is_some() {
            auto_select_hosts(&state.probed_hosts, &opts)
        } else {
            run_host_selection(&state.probed_hosts, &existing)?
        };
        
        if selected.is_empty() {
            println!("  No hosts selected. Setup cancelled.");
            state.clear()?;
            return Ok(());
        }
        
        state.selected_hosts = selected.clone();
        state.selection_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
        selected
    } else {
        state.selected_hosts.clone()
    };
    
    // Phase 4: Installation
    if !opts.skip_install && !state.installation_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 4: Installing cass");
        
        let needs_install: Vec<_> = selected.iter()
            .filter(|h| !h.cass_status.is_installed())
            .collect();
        
        if !needs_install.is_empty() {
            if opts.dry_run {
                println!("  Would install cass on {} hosts:", needs_install.len());
                for host in &needs_install {
                    println!("    - {} via {:?}", host.host_name, 
                             choose_install_method(host));
                }
            } else {
                if !opts.non_interactive {
                    let confirm = Confirm::new()
                        .with_prompt(format!(
                            "Install cass on {} hosts? (est. {} min)", 
                            needs_install.len(),
                            estimate_install_time(&needs_install)
                        ))
                        .interact()?;
                    if !confirm {
                        println!("  Skipping installation.");
                        needs_install.clear();
                    }
                }
                
                for host in needs_install {
                    state.current_operation = Some(format!("Installing on {}", host.host_name));
                    state.save()?;
                    
                    install_cass_on_host(host, |progress| {
                        display_install_progress(&progress);
                    })?;
                    
                    state.completed_installs.push(host.host_name.clone());
                    state.save()?;
                }
            }
        }
        state.installation_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
    }
    
    // Phase 5: Indexing
    if !opts.skip_index && !state.indexing_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 5: Indexing sessions");
        
        let needs_index: Vec<_> = selected.iter()
            .filter(|h| !h.cass_status.is_indexed())
            .collect();
        
        if !needs_index.is_empty() {
            if opts.dry_run {
                println!("  Would index sessions on {} hosts", needs_index.len());
            } else {
                for host in needs_index {
                    state.current_operation = Some(format!("Indexing on {}", host.host_name));
                    state.save()?;
                    
                    run_remote_index(host, |progress| {
                        display_index_progress(&progress);
                    })?;
                    
                    state.completed_indexes.push(host.host_name.clone());
                    state.save()?;
                }
            }
        }
        state.indexing_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
    }
    
    // Phase 6: Configuration
    if !state.configuration_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 6: Configuring sources");
        
        if opts.dry_run {
            println!("  Would add {} sources to ~/.config/cass/sources.toml:", 
                     selected.len());
            for host in &selected {
                println!("    - {}", host.host_name);
            }
        } else {
            let mut config = SourcesConfig::load()?;
            for host in &selected {
                let source = generate_source_config(host);
                match config.merge_source(source) {
                    MergeResult::Added(s) => println!("  ✓ Added {}", s.name),
                    MergeResult::AlreadyExists(_) => println!("  ═ {} already configured", host.host_name),
                }
            }
            config.save()?;
        }
        state.configuration_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
    }
    
    // Phase 7: Initial Sync
    if !opts.skip_sync && !opts.dry_run && !state.sync_complete {
        print_phase_header("Phase 7: Syncing data");
        
        run_sources_sync(
            Some(selected.iter().map(|h| h.host_name.clone()).collect()),
            false, // no_index (we already indexed)
            opts.verbose,
            false, // dry_run
            opts.json,
        )?;
        
        state.sync_complete = true;
        state.save()?;
    }
    
    // Phase 8: Summary
    print_phase_header("Setup Complete");
    state.clear()?;  // Clear saved state on success
    
    if opts.dry_run {
        println!("\n  Dry run complete. No changes were made.");
        println!("  Run without --dry-run to execute setup.");
    } else {
        let total_sessions: u64 = selected.iter()
            .filter_map(|h| h.cass_status.session_count())
            .sum();
        
        println!("\n  ✓ {} sources configured", selected.len());
        println!("  ✓ {} sessions now searchable", total_sessions);
        println!("\n  Run 'cass search <query>' to search across all machines");
    }
    
    Ok(())
}
```

### Progress State Persistence
For resume capability after interruption:
```rust
#[derive(Serialize, Deserialize, Default)]
pub struct SetupState {
    pub discovery_complete: bool,
    pub discovered_hosts: usize,
    pub probing_complete: bool,
    pub probed_hosts: Vec<HostProbeResult>,
    pub selection_complete: bool,
    pub selected_hosts: Vec<HostProbeResult>,
    pub installation_complete: bool,
    pub completed_installs: Vec<String>,
    pub indexing_complete: bool,
    pub completed_indexes: Vec<String>,
    pub configuration_complete: bool,
    pub sync_complete: bool,
    pub current_operation: Option<String>,
    pub started_at: Option<String>,
}

impl SetupState {
    fn path() -> PathBuf {
        dirs::cache_dir()
            .unwrap_or_default()
            .join("cass")
            .join("setup_state.json")
    }
    
    pub fn load() -> Result<Option<Self>, Error> {
        let path = Self::path();
        if path.exists() {
            let content = fs::read_to_string(&path)?;
            Ok(Some(serde_json::from_str(&content)?))
        } else {
            Ok(None)
        }
    }
    
    pub fn save(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        let path = Self::path();
        fs::create_dir_all(path.parent().unwrap())?;
        fs::write(&path, serde_json::to_string_pretty(self)?)?;
        Ok(())
    }
    
    pub fn clear(&self) -> Result<(), Error> {
        let path = Self::path();
        if path.exists() {
            fs::remove_file(&path)?;
        }
        Ok(())
    }
}
```

### Ctrl+C Handling
```rust
// Install signal handler
ctrlc::set_handler(move || {
    eprintln!("\n\n⚠ Setup interrupted. Progress has been saved.");
    eprintln!("  Run 'cass sources setup --resume' to continue.");
    std::process::exit(130);
})?;
```

- [ ] --dry-run shows what would happen without changes

- [ ] --verbose shows detailed output

- [ ] Summary shows total sessions searchable

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-dcle","title":"[Task] Opt 8.2: Implement streaming indexing with channels","description":"## Objective
Implement the streaming indexing architecture with crossbeam channels.

## Implementation Details

### Core Types
```rust
use crossbeam_channel::{bounded, Sender, Receiver, SendError, RecvError};

enum IndexMessage {
    Batch(ConversationBatch),
    Error(anyhow::Error),
    Done,
}

struct StreamingIndexer {
    channel_size: usize,
}
```

### Producer Implementation
```rust
fn spawn_connector_producer(
    connector: Box<dyn Connector>,
    tx: Sender<IndexMessage>,
) -> JoinHandle<()> {
    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        for batch in connector.pending_batches() {
            match tx.send(IndexMessage::Batch(batch)) {
                Ok(()) => {}
                Err(SendError(_)) => break,  // Consumer dropped
            }
        }
        let _ = tx.send(IndexMessage::Done);
    })
}
```

### Consumer Implementation
```rust
fn run_consumer(rx: Receiver<IndexMessage>, indexer: &mut Indexer) -> Result<()> {
    let mut active_producers = num_connectors;
    
    loop {
        match rx.recv() {
            Ok(IndexMessage::Batch(batch)) => {
                indexer.ingest(batch)?;
            }
            Ok(IndexMessage::Error(e)) => return Err(e),
            Ok(IndexMessage::Done) => {
                active_producers -= 1;
                if active_producers == 0 { break; }
            }
            Err(RecvError) => break,
        }
    }
    Ok(())
}
```

### Integration
- Replace batch collection in main indexing path
- Add feature flag check at entry point
- Preserve existing metrics/logging

## Thread Safety
- Sender is Clone, safe to share
- Receiver is single-consumer
- IndexMessage must be Send

## Rollback
Check `CASS_STREAMING_INDEX` env var at startup, use old path if "0".

## Parent Feature

Added IndexMessage enum, spawn_connector_producer, run_streaming_consumer, and run_streaming_index functions. Feature flag via CASS_STREAMING_INDEX env var (enabled by default).  {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-decq","title":"[Task] Opt 8.3: Add streaming indexing tests","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive tests for streaming indexing correctness and equivalence.

## Test Categories

### 1. Search Result Equivalence
```rust
#[test]
fn test_streaming_search_equivalence() {
    let corpus = load_test_corpus();
    
    // Index with batch mode
    std::env::set_var("CASS_STREAMING_INDEX", "0");
    let index1 = index_corpus(&corpus);
    
    // Index with streaming mode
    std::env::remove_var("CASS_STREAMING_INDEX");
    let index2 = index_corpus(&corpus);
    
    // Run same queries, compare results
    for query in test_queries() {
        let r1 = search(&index1, &query);
        let r2 = search(&index2, &query);
        assert_eq!(r1.hits, r2.hits, "Mismatch for query: {}", query);
    }
}
```

### 2. Ordering Tests
- Same corpus indexed twice → identical search results
- Deterministic tie-breaking verified

### 3. Backpressure Tests
- Verify channel fills and blocks producers when consumer is slow
- No dropped batches under pressure
- Clean shutdown when channel closes

### 4. Error Propagation Tests
- Producer error surfaces to main thread
- Consumer error stops all producers
- Partial indexing doesn't corrupt state

### 5. Stress Tests
- Large corpus (10k+ conversations)
- Many connectors (10+)
- Slow consumer (artificial delay)

+    // =====================================================================
+    // Analytics query methods
+    // =====================================================================
+
+    /// Get session count for a date range using materialized stats.
+    /// Returns (count, is_from_cache) where is_from_cache is true if from daily_stats.
+    ///
+    /// Falls back to COUNT(*) query when daily_stats table is empty or stale.
+    pub fn count_sessions_in_range(
+        &self,
+        start_ts_ms: Option<i64>,
+        end_ts_ms: Option<i64>,
+        agent_slug: Option<&str>,
+        source_id: Option<&str>,
+    ) -> Result<(i64, bool)> {
+        let agent = agent_slug.unwrap_or("all");
+        let source = source_id.unwrap_or("all");
+
+        // Check if we have materialized stats
+        let stats_count: i64 = self
+            .conn
+            .query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM daily_stats",
+                fparams![],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )
+            .unwrap_or(0);
+
+        if stats_count == 0 {
+            return self.count_sessions_direct(start_ts_ms, end_ts_ms, agent_slug, source_id);
+        }
+
+        // Use materialized stats
+        let start_day = start_ts_ms.map(Self::day_id_from_millis);
+        let end_day = end_ts_ms.map(Self::day_id_from_millis);
+
+        let count: i64 = match (start_day, end_day) {
+            (Some(start), Some(end)) => self.conn.query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COALESCE(SUM(session_count), 0) FROM daily_stats
+                 WHERE day_id BETWEEN ?1 AND ?2 AND agent_slug = ?3 AND source_id = ?4",
+                fparams![start, end, agent, source],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )?,
+            (Some(start), None) => self.conn.query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COALESCE(SUM(session_count), 0) FROM daily_stats
+                 WHERE day_id >= ?1 AND agent_slug = ?2 AND source_id = ?3",
+                fparams![start, agent, source],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )?,
+            (None, Some(end)) => self.conn.query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COALESCE(SUM(session_count), 0) FROM daily_stats
+                 WHERE day_id <= ?1 AND agent_slug = ?2 AND source_id = ?3",
+                fparams![end, agent, source],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )?,
+            (None, None) => self.conn.query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COALESCE(SUM(session_count), 0) FROM daily_stats
+                 WHERE agent_slug = ?1 AND source_id = ?2",
+                fparams![agent, source],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )?,
+        };
+
+        Ok((count, true))
+    }
+
+    /// Direct COUNT(*) query as fallback when daily_stats is empty.
+    fn count_sessions_direct(
+        &self,
+        start_ts_ms: Option<i64>,
+        end_ts_ms: Option<i64>,
+        agent_slug: Option<&str>,
+        source_id: Option<&str>,
+    ) -> Result<(i64, bool)> {
+        // Build dynamic SQL with positional params
+        let mut sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations c
+                       JOIN agents a ON c.agent_id = a.id WHERE 1=1"
+            .to_string();
+        let mut param_values: Vec<ParamValue> = Vec::new();
+        let mut idx = 1;
+
+        if let Some(start) = start_ts_ms {
+            sql.push_str(&format!(" AND c.started_at >= ?{idx}"));
+            param_values.push(ParamValue::from(start));
+            idx += 1;
+        }
+        if let Some(end) = end_ts_ms {
+            sql.push_str(&format!(" AND c.started_at <= ?{idx}"));
+            param_values.push(ParamValue::from(end));
+            idx += 1;
+        }
+        if let Some(agent) = agent_slug
+            && agent != "all"
+        {
+            sql.push_str(&format!(" AND a.slug = ?{idx}"));
+            param_values.push(ParamValue::from(agent));
+            idx += 1;
+        }
+        if let Some(source) = source_id
+            && source != "all"
+        {
+            sql.push_str(&format!(" AND c.source_id = ?{idx}"));
+            param_values.push(ParamValue::from(source));
+            let _ = idx; // suppress unused warning
+        }
+
+        let count: i64 = self
+            .conn
+            .query_row_map(&sql, &param_values, |row| row.get_typed(0))?;
+        Ok((count, false))
+    }
+
+    /// Get daily histogram data for a date range.
+    pub fn get_daily_histogram(
+        &self,
+        start_ts_ms: i64,
+        end_ts_ms: i64,
+        agent_slug: Option<&str>,
+        source_id: Option<&str>,
+    ) -> Result<Vec<DailyCount>> {
+        let start_day = Self::day_id_from_millis(start_ts_ms);
+        let end_day = Self::day_id_from_millis(end_ts_ms);
+        let agent = agent_slug.unwrap_or("all");
+        let source = source_id.unwrap_or("all");
+
+        let rows = self.conn.query_map_collect(
+            "SELECT day_id, session_count, message_count, total_chars
+             FROM daily_stats
+             WHERE day_id BETWEEN ?1 AND ?2 AND agent_slug = ?3 AND source_id = ?4
+             ORDER BY day_id",
+            fparams![start_day, end_day, agent, source],
+            |row| {
+                Ok(DailyCount {
+                    day_id: row.get_typed(0)?,
+                    sessions: row.get_typed(1)?,
+                    messages: row.get_typed(2)?,
+                    chars: row.get_typed(3)?,
+                })
+            },
+        )?;
+
+        Ok(rows)
+    }
+
+    /// Check health of daily stats table.
+    pub fn daily_stats_health(&self) -> Result<DailyStatsHealth> {
+        let row_count: i64 = self
+            .conn
+            .query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM daily_stats",
+                fparams![],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )
+            .unwrap_or(0);
+
+        let oldest_update: Option<i64> = self
+            .conn
+            .query_row_map(
+                "SELECT MIN(last_updated) FROM daily_stats",
+                fparams![],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )
+            .ok();
+
+        let conversation_count: i64 = self
+            .conn
+            .query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM conversations",
+                fparams![],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )
+            .unwrap_or(0);
+
+        let materialized_total: i64 = self
+            .conn
+            .query_row_map(
+                "SELECT COALESCE(SUM(session_count), 0) FROM daily_stats
+                 WHERE agent_slug = 'all' AND source_id = 'all'",
+                fparams![],
+                |row| row.get_typed(0),
+            )
+            .unwrap_or(0);
+
+        Ok(DailyStatsHealth {
+            populated: row_count > 0,
+            row_count,
+            oldest_update_ms: oldest_update,
+            conversation_count,
+            materialized_total,
+            drift: (conversation_count - materialized_total).abs(),
+        })
+    }
 }
